findings.title: Interprocedural taint: getReferenceCount() at line 196
findings.description: Tainted argument flows to sink through `getReferenceCount()`. Call at L196: `getReferenceCount(key, note.getContent())`.
findings.suggestion: Review the data flow through helper methods. Ensure input validation at the entry point.

findings.title: CWE-328: Weak cryptographic algorithm (DES) at line 218
findings.description: `getInstance("DES")` at L218. DES is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or stronger. For passwords, use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

findings.title: CWE-328: Weak cryptographic algorithm (DES) at line 222
findings.description: `getInstance("DES")` at L222. DES is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or stronger. For passwords, use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

findings.title: CWE-327: Weak cryptographic hash in `decryptPassword()`
findings.description: MD5 or SHA-1 used for hashing — vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 via MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256").

findings.title: CWE-328: Weak cryptographic algorithm (MD5) at line 13
findings.description: `getInstance("MD5")` at L13. MD5 is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or stronger. For passwords, use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

findings.title: CWE-327: Weak cryptographic hash in `getMd5Hash()`
findings.description: MD5 or SHA-1 used for hashing — vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 via MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256").

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS injection (IFDS) — tainted ['message'] at line 32
findings.description: IFDS taint analysis: ['message'] flows to CWE-79 sink.

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS injection (IFDS) — tainted ['message'] at line 32
findings.description: IFDS taint analysis: ['message'] flows to CWE-79 sink.

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS via response write() at line 32
findings.description: Response writer with dynamic content at L32: `writer.println(message)`.
findings.suggestion: HTML-encode all user-supplied data before writing to the HTTP response.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak PRNG (`Random`) in web handler at line 49
findings.description: `new Random()` at L49 in a web-facing method. Weak randomness in web endpoints enables session prediction and CSRF token bypass.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak random number generator in `onApplicationEvent()`
findings.description: java.util.Random or Math.random() used — not cryptographically secure.
